entry.title: Re: Is 'Price per User per Month' Model Possible in SAP B...
entry.content: Thanks, but as stated above : Quantity as a time unit (e.g. Month): If we define the quantity as a number of months and check the “Price by Period” option, the system correctly understands that the price should be billed monthly. However, in this case, we lose the link to the actual number of users, so there’s no way to multiply a “price per user per month” by the number of users.

entry.title: How to add extra fields as editable fields to a RAP BO?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I'm looking for a way to add additional fields to the behavior definition of a RAP BO so that we can change those fields from inside that object. To simply display data we have virtual elements in the projection layer, but in this case we want the fields to be editable. The changes could be saved to an external table via an additional save or unmanaged save, but the tricky thing here seems to be how to add these extra fields to the behavior definition?

Why this question: for example, why I'm looking into this, in HR some long texts are stored as HRPxxxx TLINE tables. Since we don't want to have the user edit these texts, truncated line per line in a table, we need to have a string field in our BO which contains the concatenated version of the HR table data.  In addition it might also be necessary to introduce an etag field for existing tables without admin data, if we want to make that BO draft enabled.

Some approaches I've been looking into so far:

- Use a custom entity as behavior definition entity: unfortunately no draft nor projection layer available with this approach and unmanaged scenario

- Do an outer join with an (empty) z-table which contains the additional fields in the view that is used by the behavior definition: this can actually work but it feels a bit creative. By implementing an edit with additional implementation (here we read the HR table, convert from TLINE tab to string, and set that value in our draft) + an additional/unmanaged save to write back the updated string as TLINE tab in the HR table.

So since my field does not literally exist in any db table, even via the full unmanaged scenario I didn't find a way to solve it in an elegant way. What I'm looking for is a recommended approach to have virtual (changeable) fields in the definition layer instead of the available read-only ones in the projection layer.

Any advice? 🙂

Thanks in advance!

KR,

Bjorn

entry.title: Re: Can't we use the same source objects if it is already...
entry.content:
We need to load ACDOCA with the relevant CDS View into our Datasphere. The initial load is too big when we try to do it for the last four years, and it always fails.

Initially, we loaded each historic year as a full load and do a delta only for the current year. However, the full loads / initials take around eight hours.

We then realised that the customer has changes in ACDOCA for previous years too, which is why it isn't enough to only have the current year as a delta.

However, regularly loading every year as a full load with an 8-hour runtime is also not a viable solution.

That's why we plan to use several replication flows, each for one year, and use the delta for historic years as well.

entry.title: Re: Is 'Price per User per Month' Model Possible in SAP ByDesign Contracts?
entry.content:
So you want something such as

  • a Sales Contract that includes:
    • A recurring product or service (e.g., "User License")
    • Quantity-based pricing (e.g., number of users)
    • Monthly billing cycle

But SAP Business ByDesign seems to require some time unit and only fixed price, so not sure this is possible 😟

entry.title: Updating Customer in SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud via OData HTTP Adapter
entry.content:
In my previous blog I describe how to create Customer in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud via HTTP adapter. In this post we will explore the UPDATE possibilities. 

I will skip the authentication part, as that is desribed in the mention blog and it follows the same pattern.

We'll again focus on the API_BUSINESS_PARTNER service, your go-to resource for all things related to business partners (which is how customers are represented in S/4HANA). The PATCH method will be our primary tool for making targeted updates.

The official documentation, which will be your good companion is provided at the SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

1. Updating Core Business Partner Information

This is where you'll modify fundamental details about your customer, such as their name.

API Endpoint: /sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/A_BusinessPartner('<CUSTOMER-NUMBER>')

Replace <CUSTOMER-NUMBER> with the actual customer ID you wish to update.

Method: PATCH

Let's say we want to update the customer's first name or last name. Then the request body will be as follows. General rule is that we are sending only data which we want to change. The rest remains intact.

Request Body (JSON):

{
    "FirstName": "FIRST NAME",
    "LastName": "LAST NAME"
}

2. Updating Sales Area Data

Sales area data is crucial for sales processes, defining how a customer interacts with your sales organization, distribution channel, and division.

API Endpoint: /sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/A_CustomerSalesArea(Customer='<CUSTOMER-NUMBER>',SalesOrganization='<SALES-ORG>',DistributionChannel='<DISTR-CHANNEL>',Division='<DIVISION>')

Example (with placeholders replaced): /sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/A_CustomerSalesArea(Customer='1000010',SalesOrganization='1000',DistributionChannel='01',Division='01')

Remember to replace <CUSTOMER-NUMBER>, <SALES-ORG>, <DISTR-CHANNEL>, and <DIVISION> with the specific values for the sales area you want to modify.

Method: PATCH

For example we want to update the Customer Group for a specific customer.

Request Body (JSON):

{
    "CustomerGroup": "<CUSTOMER-GROUP>"
}

3. Updating Customer Address

Updating addresses is a two-step process: first, you need to retrieve the AddressID, and then you can perform the update. This ensures you're targeting the correct address record, as a business partner can have multiple addresses.

Step 3a: Get Address to Extract Address ID

You'll need to query the business partner to retrieve the associated address ID.

API Endpoint (GET Request): /sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/A_BusinessPartner('<CUSTOMER-NUMBER>')$expand=to_BusinessPartnerAddress

Replace <CUSTOMER-NUMBER> with the relevant customer ID. Notice the 'expand' in the query. Without it the system would not return the address details, as it can contain a lot of data. If we want address details, we have to ask for it.

Method: GET

Explanation: This GET request will retrieve the business partner details. Within the response, you'll find the AddressID for each address record linked to the business partner. You'll need to parse this response to extract the correct AddressID for the address you intend to update.

Step 3b: Update Address

Once you have the AddressID, you can proceed with the PATCH request to update the address details.

API Endpoint: /sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/A_BusinessPartnerAddress(BusinessPartner='<CUSTOMER-NUMBER>',AddressID=<ADDRESS-ID>')

Method: PATCH

Again, we are sending only data which we want to change. Notice that we are calling different API (A_BusinessPartnerAddress) than when we were requesting the address object (A_BusinessPartner). This can be misleading and one of the reasons I am writing this blog to clarify it.
The body to change the whole address can look like this.

Request Body (JSON):

{
    "Country": "<COUNTRY>",
    "CityName": "<CITY>",
    "StreetName": "<STREET>",
    "HouseNumber": "<HOUSE-NUMBER>",
    "PostalCode": "<ZIP-CODE>"
}

4. Updating Customer Email

Updating email is again a two-step process similar to address update, but it gets even trickier, that's why I want to demonstrate it as well.

Step 4a: Get Current Email Address 

API Endpoint
/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/A_BusinessPartner('${property._CustomerNumber}')$expand=to_BusinessPartnerAddress/to_EmailAddress

Notice that we are again expanding the query, but this time even furhter, as we need the email address, which is nested under the general address.
This time we need to extract more parameters - AddressID, Person and OrdinalNumber !

Step 4b: Update Email Address

With all this data, we can finally run our update query. 

API Endpoint:
/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/A_AddressEmailAddress(AddressID='<ADDRESS-ID>',Person='<PERSON>',OrdinalNumber='<ORDINAL-NUMBER>')

Method: PATCH

Request Body (JSON):

{
    "EmailAddress": "NEW@EMAIL.COM"
}

That's it

Similarly, we can update phone or any other business partner object. Always ensure that you are expanding properly when calling the GET query, so that the object you want to get is listed in the response.

Recommendable is also to handle the response to the PATCH request, the desirable response code is 204. Anything else we can route as an exception in our iflow, as the update did not go through.

See also Creating Customer in SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud via OData HTTP Adapter

entry.title: Re: "How to offset a credit note during the SEPA SDD ITAL...
entry.content:
Hi,

If you are looking for netting off in F110 ( Debits and Credits for particular Vendor open items). Please make sure below settings are there -

1) Invoice reference number in Credit Memo document.

2) Currency of both Invoice and Credit Memo should be same

3) Payment method should be same for both Invoice and Credit Memo.

4) House Bank should be same.

 

Please check this and re run. It should work. Please check and confirm.

 

Regards

Vijaykumar

entry.title: New SAP Design System Portal for SAP Fiori Design Guidelines, and New UI Kits for Designers
entry.content:
Good news for creators! The SAP Fiori design guidelines site is now the SAP Design System portal. The new portal is your one-stop-shop to find everything about the design system. And that’s not all: we have released comprehensive new UI Kits for designers in Figma, the tool used by designers around the world.

 

SAP Design System Portal

The new SAP Design System portal brings together the product design guidelines SAP Fiori for web, SAP Fiori for iOS, and SAP Fiori for Android, as well as to the new digital design system guidelines. Bringing these all together is not the only improvement we’ve made, we have also:

  • Enhanced usability: easier to navigate and find what you need.
  • Improved performance: much faster page load times.
  • Simplified versioning: the 50+ guideline versions of the past for SAP Fiori for web have now been consolidated into six versions, corresponding to the six versions of SAPUI5 which are in long-term maintenance.
  • Product specific content: in addition to content which is applicable everywhere, we now also make it clear when content applies only to specific products, for example due to dependencies to product-specific technologies. At the moment we have one such section in the portal, for SAP S/4HANA (which also covers SAP Cloud ERP and SAP Cloud ERP Private).
  • Feedback button: you can now give us direct feedback on the content, via the feedback button available on the bottom right of each page (as you can see in Figure 1).

SAP’s digital design system reflects our evolved brand and is aligned to our product design system. It serves as a reference for all who contribute content to our public-facing web sites.

Figure 1: The SAP Design System portal start page. Alt Text: Image with header saying “SAP Design System”, with a “See what’s new” button below it, and then four subsections in a horizontal row: SAP Fiori for Web, SAP Fiori for Android, SAP Fiori for iOS and SAP’s Digitial Design System. A “Feedback” button is on the bottom right.

Have a look at this 45 second video to get an overview:

 

 

UI Kits for Designers

The new UI Kits available for designers in Figma make it easy for designers to design accessible UIs at enterprise scale, for web applications, mobile iOS and Android applications as well as wearables. The UI Kits provide reusable components for web and mobile, along with web floorplans, which give you what you need out-of-the-box.

In addition, we give you design resources for designing for accessibility and for performance.

Figure 2: SAP’s UI Kits for Designers in Figma. Alt-Text: The image shows two rows of three cards representing UI Kits: SAP Fiori for Android, SAP Fiori for Web, Designing for Performance, SAP Fiori for iOS, SAP Fiori for watchOS, and SAP Fiori for Wear OS. On the very left of the image information about SAP is listed, with links to resources such as www.sap.com/design-system.

Get an overview and see how easy it is for designers to put together a new UI in this 43 second video:

 

 

User Experience in SAP Community

I’m looking forward to seeing how you like the new SAP Design System portal and the new UI Kits for Designers. Do use the SAP Community to exchange your ideas and best practices by using the “User Experience” tag.

Here is a link to the latest posts for User Experience.

entry.title: RAP modify entity returns no error on failed Sales Order update (messages in es_reported are ignored
entry.content:
I’m working with a RAP-based modify entity call for a Sales Order. The issue is that even when the change is not successful, the framework returns no error – both the failed and reported parameters remain empty.

 

However, I debugged the call and noticed that inside the lhl_header=>modify method, messages are being correctly added to es_reported. It seems like these messages are not being transferred back to the top-level RAP framework and are not visible in the modify entity response.

 

For context: the modify entity call is triggered from within a custom ABAP report (not via UI or service consumption)

Could someone help me understand why the messages from es_reported are not propagated and why no error or feedback is shown even when the modification fails?

MODIFY ENTITIES OF i_salesordertp
ENTITY salesorder
UPDATE FIELDS ( ... )
WITH VALUE #( ... )
REPORTED DATA(reported)
FAILED DATA(failed).

IF failed IS INITIAL AND reported IS INITIAL.
WRITE: 'No feedback returned, but update failed in backend'.
ENDIF.

Thanks in advance,
Jalal

entry.title: Re: Onboarding New Hires Authentication using SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS)
entry.content:
Hi @josealbertova 

Unfortunately, we still not have found any solution on this for now.

Our candidates (considered as new hires at first) receive the first welcome email from IAS and they log in to do the personal data collection step. If they happen to be previous employees (so considered a rehire), they don't receive any new welcome message after that from IAS again that could mention them their login info might have changed because of the fact that they are rehired through onboarding.

For now, we only expect candidates to log in and provide their personal data so it's not yet an issue for us = We don't need them to have to come back to the external portal to do any specific tasks prior their arrival.

But when we will have tasks, I guess we will need to find a solution for this. I hope it makes sense.

Best, 

Alex

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports 2025 UFL naming conventions?
entry.content:
Hi Anders,

If you look in the x86 or x64 folder you'll find this dll:

uf12manager.dll and u2lcom.dll which are CR's C++ and COM UFL interface dll's.

uf12manager.dll is for C++/C# UFL's and it specifically looks for custom UFL and the dll must begin with u212 so the interface will load them.

Same for COM UFL dll's, they must begin with u25.

So not sure why your U25C128.dll doesn't load, possibly because it is a COM dll and you did not register using regsvr32.exe it? if you try registering it you'll get an error that it's not a COM dll if it fails to register it.

I believe also the older COM UFL's had to begin with CRUFL.... and for C++ they were u2l.... for those to be loaded by CR.

I any case it's CR's UFL Managers that are specifically looking for any dll beginning with u212 and for COM they should start with U25... will attempt to load it. Also note in the sample you do need to set CR Designers Framework in the ini file so CR knows what flavour of .NET your UFL is using.

Hope that's clear as mud... lol

Don

entry.title: Re: Mass upload requirement template link with tasks
entry.content:
Hi, Rayane,

No, you are not allowed to combine the two types of records: upload and create.  The xlsx must be one or the other.

When you download a file, you have the option to create the download with "For update" or "For Creation using Template".  

When you try to upload an xlsx created from export (download) in Cloud ALM's Requirements app, the app knows that the file is 'For update'. The upload will modify the existing requirements already existing in Cloud ALM. You can expect a pop-up such as:


When you try to upload an xlsx created from an export (download) in Cloud ALM's Requirements app, the app knows that the file is "For Creation using Template".  It will 'add' new requirements for each row, so long as there is no error in its contents.  You can expect a pop-up such as:

Best regards,
Paula

entry.title: SAP User Experience in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
January 24 marked the general availability of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release. As a cornerstone of modern enterprise, SAP Business Suite empowers organizations to scale, innovate, and adapt faster than ever. SAP cloud ERP applications, SAP Business AI, and SAP Business Data Cloud come together to deliver exceptional business value. But even the most robust cloud ERP wouldn't reach its full potential without a smooth user experience. That’s why we’re committed to evolving our product, listening to your feedback, and pushing the boundaries to deliver a user experience that matches the pace of today's dynamic work environment. Let me show you just how we achieve this in this release. Honorary mention: Generative AI is giving main character energy in this release.

Check out my video below to get a first glimpse into what’s coming in the area of SAP User Experience in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502:

 

Joule, the AI Copilot

Following a successful early adoption phase, Joule is generally available and fully integrated in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. This AI copilot utilizes generative AI to help users complete tasks more efficiently by using natural language to express their business needs. It offers quick navigation to applications, instant insights into key business data like purchase orders, and concise user enablement content to speed up tasks. Joule also simplifies personalization of the My Home section, allowing users to create cards with critical data for immediate insights. Its capabilities include recommending relevant applications, providing insightful information on business situations without needing to open the application, and offering brief summaries of help content. Users can directly create and add preview cards to the Insights section of My Home.

 

 

AI-Assisted Smart Summarization

AI-assisted smart summarization allows you to summarize object pages of SAP Fiori elements-based applications with the power of generative AI. The summarization can be reviewed and modified in a rich text editor and easily copied into productivity tools, such as email or chat, for follow-ups with co-workers and customers. The tool also facilitates efficient sharing through seamless copy-to-clipboard functionality for quick follow-ups. Additionally, users have the option to provide feedback on their experience with the feature, contributing to its continual improvement and adaptation to user needs.

 

 

AI-Assisted Easy Filter

The AI-assisted easy filter leverages state-of-the-art generative AI to redefine how users approach filtering within SAP Fiori elements-based list reports. Rather than setting filter values manually through type-ahead inputs and value helps, you can simply type your search query into the filter field. This approach not only enhances the user experience but also provides easy access to recent and favorite filter queries. The AI results are reliable, offering an intuitive and click-efficient experience, which is particularly beneficial for new users navigating the system.

 

 

Currently in Beta Testing: AI-Assisted Easy Enterprise Search

The AI-assisted easy enterprise search leverages generative AI to enable natural language querying for business objects throughout the solution. By simply typing your search query into the search field of the SAP Fiori launchpad, users can quickly access the information they need. This feature is especially valuable for its time-saving capabilities when searching with known criteria, providing reliable AI results and offering an intuitive, click-efficient experience that benefits new users in particular.

Register here to start testing: Newest AI-assisted user experience features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

 

Currently in Beta Testing: AI-Assisted Smart Personalization of My Home

The AI-assisted smart personalization of My Home allows you to use natural language through the power of generative AI to add insights cards to your My Home experience. This feature streamlines the process by enabling you to add insights cards directly from My Home with just two clicks, including a preview step for the end-user to review the AI-generated result. This tool offers several benefits, such as saving time when adding insights cards, providing better insights into business data, and delivering reliable AI results. It is designed to be intuitive and click-efficient, making it especially valuable for new users.

Register here to start testing: Newest AI-assisted user experience features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

 

Currently in Beta Testing: AI-Assisted Error Explanation

AI-assisted error explanation supports in understanding errors in SAP Fiori elements-based applications better and provides first recommendations on how to solve the errors by leveraging the power of generative AI. This innovative feature is seamlessly integrated into the current error visualization, enabling users to generate explanations on demand, which significantly saves time and improves workflow efficiency. By offering intuitive navigation, it delivers both the explanatory insight and recommended solutions within a single, click-efficient action. The process includes well-structured results that clarify the nature of the error and suggest practical resolutions.

Register here to start testing: Newest AI-assisted user experience features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

 

Interested in all things AI? Read this blog posts by my colleagues Yannick Peterschmitt and Sang Dao:

  • Artificial Intelligence and Other Intelligent ERP Use Cases in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
  • Artificial Intelligence & Technologies in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502

 

To-Dos in Microsoft Teams Integration

The integration of To-Dos in Microsoft Teams allows users to seamlessly access their workflow items from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition directly through the SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Microsoft Teams app. When you receive a workflow item in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, it triggers a notification in Microsoft Teams. This integration enables you to open the My Inbox application from Microsoft Teams to view details of the workflow items and to take immediate action. It also supports mobile approvals, ensuring flexibility in managing workflows on the go.

 

Smart Heads-Up of Situations in To Dos

Smart heads-up in To Dos ensure that you're always up to speed on situations requiring your attention, allowing you to manage all critical matters efficiently. This feature provides immediate insight and actionability through an intuitive interface, ensuring that nothing important slip through the cracks. By staying informed about and addressing potential issues early on, it significantly boosts productivity. Additionally, it offers support in prioritizing actions effectively. Users can get an overview of situations on both the standard and extended frameworks directly in the To-Dos section. With just one click, they can access detailed information by navigating to the My Situations and My Situations – Extended apps, depending on their needs.

 

As you can see, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 not only promises a transformed user experience but also enhances organizational productivity and strategic decision-making, bringing you closer to achieving seamless operational excellence, rooted in innovative technology.

Find out more about SAP User Experience in the numerous blog posts by my colleagues Thomas Reiss and Conrad Bernal.

The next release is just around the corner! See you in a bit. 😊

_____________________

The Future of Business, Today: Get Your Access to AI-Assisted Features Thanks to this Limited Promotion Now! 

The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 update is a strategic investment that combines advanced AI with powerful enterprise management tools. Whether scaling your business, enhancing productivity, or building sustainable operations, its capabilities help support a more agile and resilient future.

Sign up for following features through the limited Promotion for AI-assisted Features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (for all countries, customers, and partners):

  • The easy filter and smart summarization features in SAP Fiori allow for efficient, natural language filtering of business data and seamless summarization capabilities, enabling users to quickly extract relevant information and jump-start subsequent collaboration. 
  • Facilitate easier understanding and interpretation of financial data with insights from cost center reviews and explanations of fixed-asset depreciation keys.

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts.

Feel free to follow me on SAP Community and on LinkedIn to not miss any updates.

📽️ Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series!

From January 21 to January 24, we hosted a series of 21 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find a 43-minute-long recording and a presentation for SAP User Experience as well as 20 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime.

🔎Find All Our Assets

GROW with SAP is a comprehensive package designed to help businesses of all sizes to successfully adopt cloud ERP, anchored by SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for optimized processes and intelligent, sustainable operations. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Re: Missing Menu bar icons in Personas 3.0 after Kernel U...
entry.content:
You say that this happened "after kernel upgrade", but then you mention that there was a Personas upgrade to SP19. Was it both? 

Just asking because upgrading Personas to SP19 will not cause something like losing the icons on the screen. Rendering the flavor happens via webgui / ITS, so in such a case, a kernel or basis issue is suspect. Perhaps clearing the MIME cache may make a difference, at least it's worth trying. Also, try disabling Personas via URL parameter sap-personas-runmode=0 to use plain webgui and see if the problem is still present (which is my expectation).

Based on an earlier similar support case, upgrading the basis SP could help, along with the latest applicable kernel patch. However, in this situation you may want to create a support incident directed to component BC-FES-ITS (not BC-PER) to receive expert advice from the responsible component's support team.

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports 2011 vulns/eol/eos
entry.content:
Wow... You need to discuss this with that Vendor and why they have not updated to something more recent, even CR 2016 ended life last year. 64 bit versions are the only ones supported now.

CR 2011 support end in 2015:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/crystalreports/3354088411.html

One of the biggest issues is CR 2011 used a Java file to handle Parameter prompts, current versions now use HTML5 to handle prompts. Not to mention all of the third party dependencies have all end life as well. CR also wrote their own Charting dll, no longer uses 3Dgraphics as the chart generator.

As as you noted any CVE's that are up to date in current versions would still be vulnerable in CR 2011...

I suggest you ask them if CR 2025 can be used, there is absolutely no way to get CR 2011 now.

Issues if they install any custom User Function Libraries (UFL's), those will need to be updated to 64 bit to be able to be used in CR 2020 or 2025.

Technically any CR 2011 report, or any RPT file will load in any version of CR Designer, exceptions for CR 2025 RPT format may not be able to open in CR 2011 due to missing dependencies in CR 2011....

If their product uses any of the CR SDK's, .NET or Java, then that could cause issues also, in which case once again they need to update to current versions....

I understand that for some third party vendors wrote their applications based on specific version and the now longer maintain or able to bring the software up to date due to the developers are no longer employed but that's not your problem.... They really need to update their software...

Good luck

entry.title: DocSearch.DocStatus not correctly filtering results?
entry.content:
DocSearch docSearch = new DocSearch();
            docSearch.DocType = "SED";
            docSearch.DocNumber = "50194005";
            docSearch.DocStatus = "40";
            docSearch.ShowError = true;
            docSearch.MaxHits = 100;
           
            PlmRequestPartList ListParts = new PlmRequestPartList();
            IPlmResponse plmResponse = conn.SearchDocument(ListParts, docSearch, Com.Dscsag.Plm.Comm.SelectionMode.MULTI);
          
            string partFileName = plmResponse.Parts[0].Filename;

I trying to use this snippet of code to search for a document record, but I'm running into a snag with the status.

Based on the documents in our system, I would expect the above snippet to return version 02, as that document record is in status 40. However, when I run the snippet, plmResponse instead returns 2 results pointing at version 03, instead of version 02 as expected:

I get similiar results when searching for any other document with multiple versions, where it only returns the most recently create version, even if that version is in a 'cancelled' or 'obsolete' status.

Any suggestions for how to get the correct version when searching for documents using this API?

entry.title: SAP -Generative AI
entry.content:
In today’s fast-evolving digital economy, businesses are increasingly seeking ways to enhance productivity, improve decision-making, and deliver personalized experiences. Artificial Intelligence (AI) has emerged as a cornerstone in this transformation, and SAP is at the forefront, embedding AI directly into its business processes through SAP Business AI.

Release of ChatGPT by OpenAI has changed everyone’s perception about AI and especially Generative AI. Generative AI is disrupting the entire world and SAP is no exception. It has dramatically changed SAP ecosystem, how business run, how decisions are made and how processes are optimized. From last couple of years, SAP is investing aggressively in AI, embedding generative AI capabilities into various SAP solutions as well as SAP BTP.

In this blog series, we will learn:

How SAP is leveraging AI and generative AI across it’s portfolio. What SAP Business AI is and how SAP partners and customers can use it? What is SAP AI Core? What is SAP AI launchpad? What is Joule? etc. What role SAP BTP plays in SAP's AI strategy? ... and many more.

 

To make it easy to understand, the blog series is divided in small parts. Each blog requires maximum 10–15 minutes to learn.

 

 

 

This is the 1st blog in the series, where will understand SAP’s AI strategy. We will also learn what SAP Business AI is and why it a game changer for SAP partners and customers.

Note: I will publish the subsequent  blogs soon.

 

Target audience

Anyone who wants to learn AI capabilities and solutions provided by SAP and use that to build your own AI applications.

 

Let’s start! Before going into SAP Business AI, it's important to understand few important concepts first.

What is AI?

Artificial intelligence is when machines/computers mimic the way humans think and make decisions.

AI enables computers to think as we human think.

In simple words — AI is when we enable computers to Think.

 

What is Machine Learning?

Machine Learning is:

A subset of Artificial Intelligence. Which enables machines (or computers) to learn from data and make decisions.

 

What is Deep Learning?

Can the machine learn the way we human (human brain) learn things? — This was the idea behind innovation of Deep Learning.

Deep learning is a subset of Machine Learning (ML is again a subset of AI). At its core, deep learning is based on Artificial Neural Network (ANN), which is a computational models inspired by the structure and functioning of the human brain.

 

What is Generative AI?

Generative AI is:

A type of artificial intelligence that can create new things, for example artwork, music, or even realistic images. without being explicitly told what to create

While traditional AI focuses on specific tasks or solving a problem, Generative AI is distinguished by its ability to exhibit creativity similar to human creativity. Generative AI is capable of generating new, unique content, ideas, or solutions as we human do.

 

To know more about these basic concepts, check Generative AI for Beginners.

 

AI Platforms Vs Embedded AI

The AI market is primarily divided into two categories – AI Platforms and Embedded AI.

AI platforms

The AI platform market consists of general-purpose AI platforms provided by niche vendors and hyperscalers. These platforms provide generic AI functional services such as speech-to-text, text-to-image, text-to-video, text-to-audio etc. These vendors provide tools and frameworks for building, deploying, and managing AI applications as well.

 

Here are some prominent vendors in this space:

Google offers TensorFlow, an open-source machine learning library that facilitates the development and deployment of AI models. Google also offers pre-trained models like BERT for natural language processing tasks.

OpenAI offers ChatGPT, DALL-E (to create images from text), Sora (to create videos from text). OpenAI also offers GPT-3 and GPT-4 via API, allowing developers to integrate natural language processing capabilities into their applications.

IBM offers Watson, which provides AI tools and applications for language processing, computer vision, and data analysis.

Nvidia offers DGX platform that combines NVIDIA's software, infrastructure, and expertise to help develop AI.

 

Embedded AI

Let’s take an example of Siri. Siri is implemented using AI. However, customers consume this AI application natively as part of iOS. Customers don’t pay additional fee, don’t sign any additional contract or don’t develop any additional stuff. – That’s what embedded AI is.

Embedded AI Application means:

Customers consume AI functionality natively as part of standard applications, They don’t develop anything, but just configure and use There is no separate contract, it’s part of existing license

Other well-known examples of embedded AI from non-SAP world are:

Alexa Prices and arrival time prediction in Uber Text prediction in MS Office 365 Product recommendations in Amazon

 

SAP’s AI Strategy - Infuse AI into Applications and Business Processes

Customers expect SAP to solve business problems more effectively and innovatively. Therefore, instead of competing in general-purpose AI platform market, SAP is focusing on using AI to solve customer’s business problems.

SAP’s AI strategy is to use AI capabilities to empower business applications and business processes.

There are 2 main pillars of this strategy:

Embedded AI in SAP applications Expose Business-centric AI services via SAP BTP

 

Let’s understand these 2 pillars in detail.

 

1. Embedded AI in SAP applications

SAP is providing embedded AI capabilities natively into SAP business applications (such as SAP S/4HANA, SuccessFactors etc.) as well as SAP BTP. These embedded AI capabilities increases the value of SAP solutions and enable customer to reap the benefit of AI without any additional contract or additional development.

With Embedded AI, IT and business customers can simply configure and use the power of AI with business context and data.

Let’s take an example to understand this better - SAP Cash Application. SAP Cash Application is a bundle of cloud microservices to automate and simplify the order-to-cash process with AI and ML technology. The application can be used inside SAP S/4HANA.

 

2. Expose Business-centric AI services via SAP BTP.

SAP is focusing on offering the same targeted AI services for custom extensions which are used to enable AI functionalities inside SAP’s own solutions. For this, SAP is providing several business relevant AI services for customers and partners available via SAP BTP.

Note: As we move along the blog series, above points will become more clear to you.

 

Now, one of the most important terms that we need to understand is – SAP Business AI. Let’s go ahead and demystify this.

 

What is SAP Business AI?

All the applications, services and tools SAP is offering in AI field, are unified under one single umbrella called - SAP Business AI.

SAP Business AI is not a single product, rather it is collection of SAP’s portfolio of AI services and AI applications.

In other words, we can say that SAP Business AI is:

Product portfolio of SAP’s AI applications and AI services To automate and optimize business processes and user experience running on and powered by SAP BTP

Below image gives a high level glimpse of SAP Business AI and it’s components.

 

Note: At this point, it’s ok if you do not understand this image completely. We will discuss this in detail in the next blog.

 

Why the name “Business” AI?

You might wonder, why the term “Business” is added.

Instead of competing with general purpose AI platform, SAP is focused on solving customer’s business problems with AI. SAP Business AI is AI built for business. It is SAP’s way forward to use AI to solve business problems and manage business process more efficiently.

 

What’s so unique about SAP Business AI?

SAP’s main differentiators are – it’s access to business data, understanding of the context of complex business processes, and deep domain and industry expertise.

Although, the general-purpose large language models are pre-trained on huge data and offer amazing opportunities, they also have limitations. For example, LLMs may rely on outdated training data and lack company-specific data and business process context.

Without the business data and context, customers get results from AI model that are more like dinner-table conversation than business-altering insights and responses.

The true value of business AI comes from knowing how to apply AI to solve specific business problems. For example:

An HR manager can use SAP AI solutions to create a job description or interview questions and do this at scale. A sales manager can use SAP AI solutions to automate data extraction from complex systems and generation of various reports. When booking a trip, employees can receive offerings that are in line with company policies just by describing their travel requirements, such as destination and dates. In warehouses, where delivery notes need to be matched with orders, SAP AI solutions can automate this process or provide detailed assistance to warehouse workers, reducing manual labor.

Another important differentiator about SAP Business AI is the security, compliance, ethics and trust. SAP AI solutions may not be the fastest or the cheapest, but it is surely very secure, and follows all the principals of AI ethics. AI ethics in SAP is not just a compliance part but the most important pillar which powers all AI solutions.

To summarize, we can say that - SAP Business AI helps customers to leverage AI, powered by their business data in a secure, compliant and ethical way.

entry.title: Re: SAL Cloud ALM - Testing Reporting
entry.content:
Hi, CristinaVasla,
I apologize for delay in response.  I am reviewing unanswered questions and came across your here.

The Analytics that are available are not customizable.  Test Execution Analytics does offer different views, for example:
Execution by Status (Initial, In Progress, Failed, Passed) with the ability to filter the contents based on many elements:


Execute Manual Test Actions counts, also offering various elements to narrow the results:


and Test Execution Distribution which can be grouped by Scope, Test Plan, Priority and also offers various elements to narrow the results:

In case you would like to write your own reports, there is a standard Analytics API you can use for this.  Find information on this at:
https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/apis/analytics-api

Best regards,
Paula

 

 


You will find varyi

entry.title: SAP Developer News May 22nd, 2025
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION 

Podcast: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2025/05/22/sap-developer-news-may-22nd-2025/ 

Live from the Sapphire Show Floor 

Enhanced SAP Build Capabilities (Part 1) 

  • SAP Joule Studio Blog post -  https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/build-deploy-and-extend-ai-agents-with-joule-studio/ba-p/14105964?source=social-Global-LinkedIn-No-BTP-Awareness-Announcement-Video&campaigncode=CRM-YA23-SMS-1941773&sprinklrid=17083384815  
  • SAP Sapphire Innovation guide – BTP - https://www.sap.com/events/sapphire/innovation-guide/business-technology-platform.html 

 

SAP Architecture Center 

  • SAP Architecture Center online https://architecture.learning.sap.com/ 

 

Enhanced SAP Build Capabilities (Part 2) 

  • SAP Sapphire Innovation guide – BTP : https://www.sap.com/events/sapphire/innovation-guide/business-technology-platform.html 
  • SAP Business Suite Pricing and Packaging : https://www.sap.com/products/business-suite/pricing.html 

 

Sapphire Special Edition 

  • SAP Sapphire Innovation Guide: https://www.sap.com/events/sapphire/innovation-guide.html 
  • SAP Sapphire 2025: Notes from Day 1 Keynote with a focus on Developers: https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-blog-posts/sap-sapphire-2025-notes-from-day-1-keynote-with-a-focus-on-developers/bc-p/14107827#M49882 

AI Updates 

  • Business AI Innovation Guide: https://www.sap.com/events/sapphire/innovation-guide/ai.html 
  • AI Foundation: https://www.sap.com/events/sapphire/innovation-guide/ai.html#new-ai-foundation-provides-an-operating-system-for-sap-business-ai 
  • Prompt Optimizer: https://www.notdiamond.ai/blog/launching-prompt-adaptation 
  • Perplexity.ai: https://www.perplexity.ai/ 
  • WalkMe: https://www.sap.com/events/sapphire/innovation-guide/business-transformation-management.html#sap-introduces-new-walkme-digital-learning-solution-and-sap-business-suite-integration 

 

CHAPTER TITLES  

0:00 Intro 

0:10 Live from the Sapphire Show Floor 

2:21 Enhanced SAP Build Capabilities (Part 1) 

5:51 SAP Architecture Center 

6:52 Enhanced SAP Build Capabilities (Part 2) 

8:28 AI Updates 

 

TRANSCRIPTION

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for May 22nd, 2025. Special Sapphire Edition.

[Josh] Hey, everybody. Josh Bentley, Mr. Bluebeard, and representing the SAP Experience Center here on the Sapphire show floor. Now, what we've got is you come in and you learn about the different roles that you can do. You can be a CIO, CFO, different, different personas associated with all the technology SAP is talking about here at Sapphire. And once you do that, we create an AirTag that works with an Android or iOS phone. it gets custom built here and attached to your device so when you go home you can continue learning what you were actually exploring here on the show floor and if we go this way we've got the newest member of the sap community i want to show them to you he's AI powered by Joule i'm sure nice little robot there android and uh we got things going on with tons of business transformation AI uh supply chain over here we have an injection machine showing you how you can actually print the parts that were shown in the keynote and then this is how they come from the machine if it breaks you get the repair part actually from the warehouse so it's all automated and it's a great demo for all the customers here this is just one section of the Sapphire show floor i'm going to sneak out now the back and skip part of the experience center and go towards the learning and certification hub where everybody can actually go through and they can learn and take some tests on site and see what the certification programs are like on the way to that which is way back in the corner you can stop by any of the vendor booths that are happening and you've got plenty of vendors that we've known for years that are great trusted partners and customers and then we've got in the middle all the applications BTP all the sections that you can go to for let's say a different theater session I was talking to Sanya Leonard earlier and her team is doing great things with Abap cloud and so much is coming with that and AI and the innovations that we're doing in that space so again it's super busy here Sapphire This is the end of day three coming up, and what I want to get out of this is that you've got a ton of energy here in the community and a lot of things going on for not just developers, for everybody at SAP, but I'm heading back there to do some more work in the SAP Learning and Certification Hub. I'll talk to you later. Bye.

[Shrinivasan] Hello everybody it's an immense pleasure to bring you all the crucial highlights from SAP Sapphire 2025 annual conference that happened over last two days in Orlando Florida let's get this thing right off the bat Joule studio has been launched as part of SAP Build Solution Portfolio. Joule Studio has two key capabilities. Number one, Skill Builder Capability. Using this, you can design custom skills and execute predefined tasks within conversations triggered contextually by user intent. These skills produce specific results. They are configurable to the customer landscape. Skills can leverage other build components such as pre -built automations access to third -party integrations and AI models this feature will be made available by end of q2 this year number two agent builder capability using this you can create custom ai agents that can execute processes and workflows with varying adaptability and self-healing capabilities. Rather than following a set of prescribed instructions, agents leverage the thinking capability aspect of LLM to figure out how to solve a problem as well as APIs to augment their solution. Grounded in customer business data and SAP knowledge graph, these agents can deliver precise outputs and business-centric outcomes accommodating human inputs throughout the workflow. This feature will be made available Q4 this year. If you would like to know more details about Joule Studio, do read this blog post by SAP BTP expert Bharat Sandhu. Link is in the description box. That's not it. SAP announces new Joule capabilities for developers in SAP Build. These AI-assisted capabilities help empower developers at any skill level to build applications more quickly and easily. 1. SAP Build Work Zone Employees can work smarter with quick access provided by Joule to relevant apps, tasks, and processes from one central place. Users can further use Joule to query documents using document grounding capabilities. create new documents, and summarize them for relevant information needs. By providing AI responses based on SAP-relevant data from SAP Build Work Zone, employees can save time and stay productive. Number two, in SAP ABAP Cloud, whether it's migrating custom ABAP ECC code to SAP Cloud ERP to maintain a clean core or adapting existing legacy code to ABAP cloud model standards, these AI-driven enhancements can help developers be more productive and deliver precise outcomes faster. These two capabilities will be made available in Q3 this year. If you need more details, kindly refer to the BTP Innovation Guide provided in the description. Keep exploring and keep learning thank you.

[DJ] there's a new online resource for folks in the SAP tech ecosphere and that is the sap architecture center think of it as a one-stop shop for reference architectures on topics as diverse as event-driven application design multi-region resiliency federated machine learning and lots more why are reference architectures so important well think best practices think standardization scalability efficiency and interoperability and the best part about the new sap architecture center it's open open for collaboration for feedback and contributions using effectively the industry standard for collaboration on documentation and that is via GitHub. I've already made my first contribution. Dive in to the SAP Architecture Sensor now. See you online.

[Sheena M K] Beyond the exciting AI advancements around its supplier, SAP Build is bringing a host of expanded capabilities to help customers seamlessly extend their SAP business suite. Let's look at what they are. An enhanced Build Lobby which acts as the central hub for creating extension projects is now generally available which makes it easier than ever to get started. Extensibility Wizard, a guided tool which simplifies the process of building on stack and side -by-side extensions was made available in S4HANA public cloud edition at the end of last year. And here is more good news, by second half of 2025 this capability will also be available for SuccessFactors solutions. Data products from SAP Business Data Cloud will be accessible directly within SAP Build, supercharging application development while giving users richer data insights. This transformative integration is set to go live in second half of 2025. SAP Build is soon to support a wider range of event sources, enabling real-time dynamic interactions across multiple systems. Expect this feature to become generally available in the second half of 2025. SAP Build's streamlined pricing lets customers extend their business suite while giving developers the flexibility to choose the right tools without the hassle of managing multiple licenses. With these powerful new capabilities, SAP Build is set to transform how businesses create and scale extensions.

[Thomas] we've already heard from some of my colleagues about other ai enhancements that were announced at sapphire this week those affecting BTP the integration suite how Joule studio is being introduced inside of SAP build for extending Joule capabilities as well as how the business Data Cloud is the foundation of bringing business data into AI and making it more intelligent. But I'd like to talk to you now about some of the announcements that came that were part of the AI foundational layer. And right there, there was an announcement of a new version, new capabilities for AI Foundation itself. We see the current capabilities of AI Core and AI Launchpad being enhanced with new features like a new agent runtime. Or another example is the prompt optimizer to help you both optimize your existing prompts, but also help you write LLM independent prompts so that you're no longer tied with your prompts and your LLM generation overall to a particular LLM and that's through a partnership with Diamond AI. We'll be bringing the prompt optimizer to the BTP AI experience. We also saw another important partnership and that's with Perplexity. This is a tool that gives you an LLM-like experience but searching current information in the web. It's also really good at citing its sources and providing those in the output and this is an experience that we plan to bring to SAP Joule in the near future as well. So that was two super exciting partnerships that are going to enhance the AI capabilities of Joule and what you can build with AI on SAP BTP. And finally, we also saw the impact that the WalkMe acquisition is having to tie together Joule capabilities across your entire business, whether that's the SAP business suite or non-SAP capabilities as well. There's some really cool demos showing how ServiceNow was integrated with Ariba and S4. And Joule was able to tie that all together through the WalkMe capabilities. And like we said the other day in our little write-up after the day one keynote, This gives you a glimpse of some of the great things that are going to come to TechEd later this year because the announcements you hear at Sapphire are often the things we'll be able to show you exactly how you as developers are going to use later this year at TechEd. So be on the lookout for all that kind of information coming later this year.

entry.title: Generative AI with SAP – Introduction to SAP Business AI
entry.content:
In today’s fast-evolving digital economy, businesses are constantly seeking ways to increase productivity, make smarter decisions, and personalize customer experiences. Artificial Intelligence (AI) has become a core driver of this change, and SAP is leading the way with SAP Business AI—a powerful suite of AI capabilities embedded directly into SAP business processes.

In this post, we’ll introduce SAP Business AI, explore Generative AI, and highlight how tools like Joule, SAP’s new AI copilot, are transforming the enterprise landscape.

What Is SAP Business AI?

SAP Business AI is SAP’s portfolio of enterprise-grade AI technologies integrated across its cloud applications and platforms. Unlike general-purpose AI, SAP Business AI is:

  • Context-aware

  • Built for business

  • Responsible by design

Key Components:

  • Embedded AI in core SAP apps like S/4HANA, SuccessFactors, and Customer Experience

  • Industry-specific AI scenarios

  • Business-centric models trained on anonymized SAP data

  • Tools for AI development, deployment, and monitoring

What Is Generative AI?

Generative AI refers to AI that can create new content—such as text, images, code, and more. Using large language models (LLMs), it can generate human-like outputs from natural language prompts.

In a business context, Generative AI powers:

  • Automated document creation (e.g., job descriptions, invoices)

  • Summarization of reports and meeting notes

  • Personalized marketing content

  • Code generation and debugging support

  • Intelligent chatbots and virtual assistants

How SAP Integrates Generative AI

SAP’s approach to Generative AI is enterprise-centric and secure.

1. SAP Joule – Your AI Copilot

Meet Joule: SAP’s new natural language copilot embedded throughout SAP’s cloud portfolio.

With Joule, users can:

  • Retrieve real-time insights

  • Automate tasks with plain-language commands

  • Converse with business data contextually

Example:
Prompt: “What are the top 5 vendors based on last quarter’s delivery performance?”
Joule: Delivers a contextual, accurate answer—instantly.

2. Strategic Partnerships with Leading AI Providers

SAP partners with OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google Cloud Vertex AI to deliver cutting-edge LLM capabilities while ensuring data security, privacy, and compliance.

3. Developer Enablement via SAP AI Core and Launchpad

  • SAP AI Core: Infrastructure for managing and deploying AI models

  • SAP AI Launchpad: Tools to monitor and govern models responsibly

These tools empower developers to integrate Generative AI into custom workflows using the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP).

Real-World Use Cases Across SAP

SAP is already using Generative AI in:

  • HR: Writing job descriptions, drafting interview questions

  • Finance: Summarizing financial reports

  • Customer Service: Enhancing chat support with smart assistants

  • Procurement: Drafting RFQs, analyzing supplier performance

  • Marketing: Creating personalized campaign content

Responsible AI by Design

SAP is committed to ethical, transparent AI use. Its approach includes:

  • Transparency – Letting users know when AI is used

  • Privacy – Ensuring anonymized, secure data handling

  • Fairness – Avoiding bias in training and outputs

Backed by SAP’s AI Ethics Policy and governance framework, customers can trust that SAP Business AI is aligned with industry best practices.

Getting Started with SAP Business AI

Ready to explore?

  • Try Joule in your SAP applications

  • Explore SAP AI Core and AI Launchpad on SAP BTP

  • Visit the SAP Discovery Center for use cases

  • Join the SAP Community to connect with experts

Generative AI is no longer just a buzzword—it’s redefining business operations. SAP Business AI helps organizations unlock AI’s potential in a responsible and familiar environment.

Deep Dive: Meet Joule

What Is Joule?

Imagine a trusted AI assistant embedded throughout your enterprise systems. That’s Joule.

It’s an intelligent, conversational AI copilot designed to:

  • Deliver contextual insights from SAP and third-party data

  • Help users complete tasks faster

  • Maintain compliance and data security standards

How Customers Use Joule

Joule will be embedded across SAP applications—HR, finance, supply chain, procurement, and more.

Example Use Cases:

  1. Sales: Analyze performance across regions and link to supply chain data

  2. HR: Generate inclusive job descriptions

  3. Finance: Automate financial summaries and forecasting

  4. Customer Support: Resolve issues faster with contextual data

Key Features of Joule

  • Conversational UI using SAP Fiori design principles

  • Out-of-the-box integration with SAP backend systems

  • Built for compliance – GDPR, SOC-II, AI ethics included

  • Always improving with automatic updates and feature rollouts

Joule in Procurement: Smarter, Faster Decisions

Procurement is a strategic function where speed and accuracy are key. Joule supercharges procurement by enabling:

Real-Time Insights

Ask: “What was our Q1 spend on IT hardware?”
Joule fetches, analyzes, and visualizes instantly.

Supplier Performance Analysis

Ask: “Which vendors had the highest late delivery rate?”
Joule uses embedded analytics to provide actionable insights.

Content Automation

  • Draft RFQs and emails

  • Propose contract language

  • Generate follow-ups automatically

Workflow Acceleration

“Show PRs needing approval”
“Approve all low-risk PRs below $5,000”
Joule speeds up approvals while maintaining controls.

Risk Detection

Ask: “Which POs are missing documentation?”
Joule flags compliance issues in real time.

Why It Matters

In procurement, Joule helps:

  • Make faster decisions

  • Improve team collaboration

  • Detect risks proactively

  • Boost operational efficiency

And it’s only getting better. Coming soon: Full lifecycle procurement support, including sourcing and supplier collaboration.

Conclusion

SAP Business AI and Generative AI are reshaping how businesses operate—responsibly, securely, and intelligently. Tools like Joule make AI accessible and actionable across all departments, helping organizations become more agile, informed, and efficient.

entry.title: Production order Release getting rejected (Incorrect Check for order XXXXXXXX)
entry.content:
Hi All

We have a scenario where background program is creating the production order. The configuration is set to release the production order immediately during creation. We have recently upgraded to S4 hana 2023 and we are seeing that the production orders are not getting released automatically now. They are getting RELR status. Since production order does not store error log we are not able to find the cause of rejection. When we are trying to release the same order via CO02 it gets release successfully.

The weird thing is if I first press on material availability button in CO02 I get error  "Incorrect Check
for order XXXXXXXX". In log text its says checking rule missing for order type and plant. But we have all the config present in OPJK. Now if we press release it gets released successfully.

Also if I am using COHV to mass release the production order some order gets fail to release and shows this material availability check error, but if we re process them they get successfully released.

If anyone has any input for this situation it would be great help
